title: Fractions, Decimals, Ratios, and Proportional Structure · Transfer Challenge
summary: Scale recipes, medication-free training examples, mixtures, maps, and staffing plans without changing relationships. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a proportional scaling and comparison workbook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent fractions as part-whole, quotient, ratio, operator, and location meanings in context.
Add, subtract, multiply, and divide fractions by reasoning about units and equivalent forms.
Convert among fractions, decimals, and terminating or repeating representations without losing meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 22 units, changes by −9 units, then by +8 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale.

The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.
